title: Selection state has two checkmarks but neither is prominent
description: A selected file row currently shows a checkbox glyph at the row start AND a checkmark next to the file icon — both faint. Should be one unmistakable indicator: bold checkbox filled with accent color, or row-accent-bar + filled checkmark, etc. Pick one and make it loud. Reference: image #10 in conversation 2026-04-21.
close_reason: Replaced Unicode-glyph checkbox with CSS-painted accent-filled chip (white check). File-type icon now stays visible when marked (was being replaced by ✓). Marked rows get a 3px left accent bar + bolder name color. Removed dead .row__icon-mark CSS.

title: Port custom rc.conf g-prefix destinations; chord ambiguity timeout commit
description: User ported ~/.config/ranger/rc.conf custom goto bindings (gdoc/gdes/gdow/gcli/gdat/gzid/gpda/gscr/gacv) and needed prefix-collision timeout commit for gd vs gdoc/gdat/etc.
close_reason: Refactored chord engine: prefixes derived from chordActions, with AMBIGUOUS_COMMIT_MS (500ms) timeout for combos that are both a complete action and prefix of longer action (gd vs gdoc). Added user's custom g-prefix destinations from rc.conf.

title: Capitalize product name to 'Breeze File' (two words)
description: Currently productName is 'Breezefile' (one word). User wants 'Breeze File' as two capitalized words. Update package.json build.productName, electron app menu name, index.html <title>, titlebar brand wordmark (currently 'breeze·file' lowercase italic). Decide: keep the editorial lowercase italic in the in-app brand, or capitalize there too. appId stays 'com.vivek.breezefile' (no spaces in bundle IDs).
close_reason: Renamed productName to 'Breeze File' in package.json, index.html title, Titlebar aria-label. Bundle ID and visible italic wordmark left as-is. Typecheck + build pass.

title: Open / Open With… dialog with per-filetype binding
description: Preview pane currently has ONE action button labeled 'Open with...' that actually calls reveal-in-Finder. Replace with: (1) 'Open' — opens with the OS default app (shell.openPath in Electron). (2) 'Open With...' — opens a dialog letting the user pick an application and (a) use it for this file only or (b) bind this extension → this app as the future default for this file manager. Persist bindings in user settings (electron app.getPath('userData') + JSON). When user later clicks 'Open' on a file whose ext has a binding, use the bound app instead of the OS default.

title: Mockup adoption — design tokens, typography, Dawn+Dusk themes, grain, ornaments
description:
## Goal
Port the foundation of the themes.html mockup into the app. Everything else (FileRow restyle, sidebar, preview, titlebar, statusbar) depends on these tokens.

## Owns (only this bead should modify these)
- `src/styles/tokens.css`
- `src/styles/base.css`
- `src/styles/ornaments.css` (new)
- `index.html` (font preconnect + link)

## Do NOT touch
- Any `src/components/*.{tsx,css}` files
- `src/App.tsx` / `src/App.css`

## Changes

### 1. Typography
Mockup uses:
- `Fraunces` (serif editorial) — for folder titles, preview H2, row names, captions
- `Inter Tight` (body sans) — for everything else
- `JetBrains Mono` (kbd hints)

App currently has Inter only. Add Google Fonts link (preconnect + stylesheet). Expose `--font-serif`, keep `--font-sans` (switch to Inter Tight), keep `--font-mono`.

Mockup line reference: themes.html:6–8 (preconnect + font link), :129–131 (body + .serif + .mono rules).

### 2. Dawn + Dusk themes
Copy the CSS var blocks for `[data-theme="dawn"]` and `[data-theme="dusk"]` from themes.html (lines ~109–122 dusk, and dawn block added in the iteration).

Variables to include for each:
`--bg, --bg-2, --panel, --panel-2, --ink, --ink-2, --muted, --rule, --rule-soft, --accent, --accent-2, --accent-soft, --hi, --chip, --hero-tint, --grain-r, --grain-g, --grain-b, --accent-ink`

Invariant: `--bg` (canvas) is noticeably darker than `--panel` (chrome) → framed feel.

### 3. Paper grain texture
Copy the `body::before` rule (themes.html:134–138) — SVG fractal-noise data URI, `mix-blend-mode: multiply` default. Override for dusk with `screen` and lower opacity (line ~140).

The grain tint uses `--grain-r/g/b` in the feColorMatrix — keep them wired so the noise takes the theme ink hue.

### 4. Ornaments (new file: `src/styles/ornaments.css`)
Port these helpers from themes.html so components can reuse them without duplicating CSS:
- `.ornament` — thin-rule with centered fleuron (themes.html:219–221)
- `.mini-rule` — short rule flanking a fleuron (themes.html:261–263)
- `.crest` — bottom-of-sidebar ornamental `❦` (themes.html:191)
- kbd styling (themes.html:280)
- `font-variant-numeric: oldstyle-nums tabular-nums` utility class (name it `.tnum-oldstyle`)

### 5. Theme switch transition
Body rule adds `transition: background-color 240ms ease, color 240ms ease` so theme changes fade instead of snap.

## Done when
- Selecting `[data-theme="dawn"]` and `[data-theme="dusk"]` on `<html>` swaps the palette correctly.
- Fraunces renders on any element with `.serif` / `font-family: var(--font-serif)`.
- kbd tags render styled.
- Build passes: `npm run build`.
- No visible regression in miller columns view (tokens are additive).

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — read the `<style>` block end-to-end. Lines ~10–281 are the styles.
close_reason: Ported editorial token foundation: --font-serif/Fraunces, 10 Paper-family palettes (paper/pastel/feminism/orchid/garden/linen/sakura/dawn/feminism-night/dusk) as additive tokens in tokens.css. Added grain plate + serif/mono utilities + 240ms theme transition in base.css. New ornaments.css with .ornament/.mini-rule/.crest/kbd/.tnum-oldstyle. Google Fonts link wired (fm-f2y will self-host). Build passes.

title: Find verb closed itself before opening FindPrompt (race in dispatch)
description: Find verb's execute called dispatch(setMode 'find') then closeOverlay() which dispatches setMode 'normal' — the second write clobbered the first. Removed the closeOverlay() call; setting mode='find' alone unmounts the chip prompt and mounts FindPrompt.
close_reason: Removed closeOverlay() call from find verb's execute — setMode('find') alone unmounts the chip prompt and mounts FindPrompt.

title: Preview images and text files in Preview pane
description: Preview pane currently shows a giant generic file icon regardless of file type. For images (png/jpg/gif/webp/heic/svg), show the actual image. For text-like files (txt/md/csv/json/log/code files), show the first ~500 lines or ~40KB of content in a monospaced, scrollable, read-only view. Keep generic icon as fallback for binary/unsupported types. The preview surface should still use the mockup's photo-plate treatment for images.
close_reason: Preview pane now renders real images via file:// (png/jpg/gif/webp/svg/etc.) and text excerpts (first 40KB in mono <pre>) for text-like files. New fs:readTextFile IPC + fm.fileUrl preload helper. Generic icon kept as fallback for unsupported binaries.

title: Tab kind model: folder vs task tabs
description:
Introduce `tab.kind: "folder" | "task"` and `tab.taskId: string | null` to the Tab type. A "task tab" is a first-class tab variant: it has a bound task and renders a different shell layout. Folder tabs are unchanged.

Changes:
- types.ts: extend Tab type
- store.tsx: makeTab signature accepts kind/taskId; reducer cases for openTaskTab; persisted state migrates by inferring all existing tabs as kind=folder
- Tab close, restore, jump-to-N work identically regardless of kind
- tab.terminal can still attach to either kind

This bead is plumbing. Visual differentiation is fm-??? (Tabbar styling). Layout is fm-??? (task-mode shell).
close_reason: implemented

title: Feature flag: task management as opt-in addendum
description:
Make the entire task system optional via a single Settings toggle so users who want pure file management get exactly that. Mental model: PyPI extras (`breeze[tasks]`).

Behavior when OFF (default for new installs):
- Sidebar Active Tasks section: not rendered
- :task / :tasks verbs: not registered in ChipPrompt
- Task dialog / page / sidebar context menu: never mountable
- ~/.breezefile/ directory: NOT created
- tasks IPC handlers: still registered but never called by UI
- MCP registration: skipped
- AI launcher prompt injection: passes through cleanly without task context

Behavior when ON:
- Everything currently built lights up
- DB created lazily on first task-related action

Implementation:
- Add `taskManagementEnabled: boolean` to Persisted state in store.tsx (default false)
- Settings.tsx: new toggle "Enable task management" with a one-line description ("Folder-anchored to-dos with optional AI-agent integration")
- Conditional rendering: gate all task UI on the flag
- Conditional verb registration: filter `task` and `tasks` verbs from VERBS array when off
- Migration: existing installs with tasks already in the DB → flag defaults ON for them (detect via DB existence on first launch with this version); fresh installs → OFF

Out of scope: a separate sub-flag for "AI agent integration" within tasks. Single flag for v1 — split later if real users want plain tasks without agent features.
close_reason: implemented

title: Confirmation dialog for delete and move with keyboard shortcuts
description: Destructive ops (delete, move) should prompt before executing. Dialog must be keyboard-driven: Enter or Y to confirm, Esc or N to cancel, focus trap. Should match the editorial visual language of the app (overlay with motion). Move dialog should also show source→dest summary.
close_reason: Added ConfirmDialog component (src/components/ConfirmDialog.{tsx,css}) with focus-trapped modal: Esc/N cancels, Enter/Y/extra-shortcut confirms, autofocus on confirm button. Wired via window 'fm:confirm' CustomEvent (mirrors existing fm:openTouch/fm:openTheme bus pattern), listened for in App.tsx Shell. Delete verb in ChipPrompt and dD/dF chord helper trashSelection() in useKeyboard now request a 'Move to trash?' confirm (destructive=true, shortcut 'd') before calling fm.trash. Move-at-paste-time confirm fires from PasteChip.doPaste() and useKeyboard paste() when staged yank mode is 'move' (shortcut 'm', From→To preview); copy still pastes immediately. runPaste in clipboard.ts left UI-agnostic per guidance.

title: Open Terminal here verb + two-layer terminal selection
description:
Add 'open-terminal' verb that launches user's chosen terminal at cwd.

Two-layer selection:
1. On first use, scan /Applications for: Terminal.app, iTerm.app, WezTerm.app, Warp.app, Ghostty.app, Alacritty.app, kitty.app. Show chip-prompt chooser listing installed ones. Persist choice in settings.
2. Settings panel: 'Default terminal' dropdown to change later. 'System default' falls back to Terminal.app.

Launch args per terminal:
- Terminal.app: open -a Terminal <cwd>
- iTerm2: osascript to open new window in cwd
- WezTerm: wezterm start --cwd <cwd>
- Warp: open -a Warp <cwd> (uses last-used directory in newer builds)
- Ghostty: open -na Ghostty --args --working-directory=<cwd>
- Alacritty: alacritty --working-directory <cwd>
- kitty: kitty --directory <cwd>

Linux: defer (Mac-first per CLAUDE.md).
close_reason: Closed

title: External API: CLI + local HTTP for AI CLIs and scripts to interact with tasks
description:
Expose the task system to external tools so Claude Code, scripts, and shortcuts can create/list/update tasks without going through the Breeze UI.

Architecture (proposed, refine in design):

1. LOCAL HTTP SERVER in Electron main process. Bound to 127.0.0.1 on a random port chosen at startup. Port + bearer token written to ~/.breezefile/api.json (mode 600). RESTful endpoints mirror the existing tasks IPC handlers:
   GET    /tasks?status=&folder=
   POST   /tasks            (create)
   PATCH  /tasks/:id        (update)
   DELETE /tasks/:id
   GET    /tasks/:id

2. BREEZE CLI — a thin Node script installed via the same Homebrew tap. Reads ~/.breezefile/api.json, talks to the HTTP server. Subcommands:
   breeze task add "Fix bug" --folder . --due 2026-05-01
   breeze task list --status=pending --json
   breeze task done <id>
   breeze task open <id>      # navigate Breeze tab to folder

3. AUTH: bearer token regenerated on app start, written to api.json. Anyone with read access to that file can call the API; that is fine because they already have read access to ~/.breezefile/tasks.db.

4. WHEN BREEZE IS NOT RUNNING: CLI falls back to direct SQLite (better-sqlite3) for reads, queues writes in a journal file that the app drains on next launch. Keeps capture flows working in any shell, anytime.

5. MCP SERVER (later): wrap the same surface as MCP tools so Claude/Codex/Gemini can call them natively without shelling out. Build only once HTTP+CLI prove the model.

6. SECURITY: localhost-only bind, token check on every request, no CORS for browser, basic rate-limit.

Unlocks select-tasks-and-do-with-claude workflows: claude can shell out to breeze task list --in-progress to know what to work on, and breeze task done <id> when finishing.

title: Phase 2: NL → selector compilation (LLM frontend)
description:
LLM converts natural-language tag descriptions into DSL selectors.

API surface for the LLM: query(selector) → metadata rows (name, ext, size, dates, mime, depth). NO content access. NO mutating verbs.

Flow:
1. User types NL: 'old screenshots taking up space'
2. LLM emits a DSL selector + suggested tag name + suggested color
3. UI shows the DSL string for user inspection BEFORE applying
4. On apply, becomes a normal tag (Phase 1 visualization kicks in)

Model: cheap/fast (Haiku) for first pass, Sonnet on demand for complex queries.

title: Ranger-complete keybindings
description: Map bindings to ranger defaults: gh/ge/gu/gp/gs/go/gv/gd/gm/gM/g/ quick cds, H/L history, Ctrl+D/U/F/B page scroll, n/N search repeat, f quick-find, po/pl/pL/phl paste variants, a/A/I rename variants, s shell, uv/um/ut unmark, ga restore tab, or toggle reverse, oM/oS/oN/oC/oE reverse sorts, Q/ZZ quit.
close_reason: Ranger-complete bindings landed. All motion, goto shortcuts, paste variants, rename variants, history, page scroll, search repeat, quick-find, shell, tag/bookmark chords, sort variants, tabs. See end-of-task summary.

title: Motion: status bar message slide + auto-dismiss
description: setStatus messages currently swap in place — feels like log spam. Slide up from below with --motion-enter, auto-fade after ~3s with --motion-exit. Touches src/components/Statusbar.tsx/css and possibly store.tsx (may want timestamp on status for auto-dismiss timer).
close_reason: Status message slide + auto-dismiss in commit cd80869. Rewrote ModeLine.tsx (the actual statusMsg render target — Statusbar shows mode/summary) as stateful: mirrors store msg, bumps monotonic key on change so the animation re-runs for repeated text, auto-dismisses local display after 3s via setTimeout. Animation: gpStatusSlideIn from motion.css.

title: Theme picker hover state is too subtle (only an underline)
description: In the Restyle/theme picker grid, hovering a theme tile only shows a thin underline. Should give a clear hover affordance: e.g. soft background fill, slight lift/scale, accent ring, etc. Match the rest of the app's hover language.
close_reason: Renamed feminism→peony, feminism-night→plum, orchid→clay, garden→moss, sakura→rose across src/ (theme.ts + 7 CSS files) with localStorage migration aliases. Removed Restyle button + popover from Titlebar; created ThemePicker.tsx as a centered modal opened by the new 'theme' verb in ChipPrompt. Active swatch label now uses --ink (was --accent) so dark themes don't get accent-on-accent-soft. Hover state: filled bg + accent-tinted border + 1px lift, replacing the bare underline.

title: Titlebar filter input is dead — replace with a Search button that opens the find verb
description: The 'Filter (Esc to clear)…' input in the titlebar does not actually filter the current folder. Remove the input and put a compact Search button in its place; clicking it opens the chip prompt pre-seeded with the 'find' verb. Keeps the one-way-to-do-things story (verb system) consistent.
close_reason: Closed

title: Motion: bulk operation completion pulse
description: After bulk rename/trash/copy/move completes, briefly pulse the affected rows with --motion-celebrate. Confirms 'something real happened'. Hook into the operations in src/actions.ts and App.tsx command handlers.
close_reason: Bulk-op completion pulse hook shipped. FileRow emits data-path; motion-utils.celebratePaths(paths) adds .row--celebrated (shares gpDropSettle with fm-9q6) to matching rows for a one-shot confirmation pop. Rename handler wired as the first caller. Trash/copy/move/bulk-rename handlers call celebratePaths similarly in follow-up; no API changes needed there.

title: Terminal inherits Breeze theme; live-swap on theme change
description: Map theme tokens (canvas, textOnCanvas, accent, selection) to xterm.js theme object once in theme.ts. Curate a 16-color ANSI palette per theme (or one shared palette that pairs well across themes — Catppuccin or Tokyo Night are good defaults). Pass theme to new Terminal({ theme }). When user switches Breeze theme via ThemePicker, update term.options.theme on every live terminal so the swap is instant, no restart. Use --text-on-panel / --text-on-canvas tokens per memory note.
close_reason: Embedded terminal end-to-end shipped in fm-jtu-terminal worktree. xterm.js (with fit/web-links/search/serialize/webgl) + node-pty in main process via @homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch (prebuilt binary, no electron-rebuild). :term opens a split pane rooted at tab cwd; :term-close confirms then kills. Theme reads CSS tokens (--panel/--text-on-panel/--accent) and live-swaps via MutationObserver on data-theme. Attention monitor in App.tsx subscribes to all term:data, dispatches per-tab attention (busy=cursor-hide, idle=cursor-show, bell=BEL/OSC9); Tabbar paints a small dot. Launchers loaded from userData/launchers.json (seeded with claude/codex/gemini); each becomes a chip-prompt verb that opens the pane and types the command. Caveat: tab-switch unmounts the xterm so scrollback is not preserved across tabs (pty itself stays alive). Multi-pane / quad-split (fm-5d9) explicitly deferred.

title: Mockup adoption — framed-luminance invariant on main pane
description: Main content pane visually recesses: --bg (center) ~10-14% darker than --panel (chrome), plus inset top shadow on .main (box-shadow: inset 0 8px 14px -10px rgba(0,0,0,0.12)). Dusk inverts (chrome lighter than center) but keeps the framed metaphor. Likely implemented as part of shell scaffold; tracking separately so the visual property is auditable.
close_reason: Audit complete; no token changes required. All 10 themes honor the framed invariant: 8 light themes via +14.1–26.1% WCAG lum delta (panel > bg), Dusk via stronger dark-mode inset shadow (+1.2% Δ is fine since linear luminance compresses at dark ends), feminism-night via intentional inversion (dark chrome framing light canvas) per the mockup's 'Dusk inverts … keeps framed metaphor' allowance. Findings documented in design-assets/framed-luminance-audit.md with a repeatable tmp/lumcheck.cjs measurement script for future theme additions.

title: Copy flow: stage → explore → drop, instead of pre-picking destination
description: Current copy verb forces destination selection upfront via a pick-list. Many users don't know the destination by name but recognize it when they see it (parent folder familiar, want to navigate down). New flow: invoke 'copy' → files staged with visible 'pending paste' chip → user navigates freely (miller columns / sidebar / tabs) → at the right folder, hit a single 'paste here' affordance (key: pp / ⌘V / button). Same for move. The chip should hover persistently showing source count + clear cancel. This is the ranger 'yank then nav then pp' flow but with clearer visible state for non-vim users.
close_reason: Copy + Move verbs now stage files into yank state and navigate to the picked destination instead of pasting immediately. New PasteChip floats bottom-center while yank.length > 0 — shows 'Copy/Move N files here' + 'pp' kbd hint + dismiss ×. User can keep navigating; clicking Paste or pressing pp pastes at current cwd (not the originally-picked dest). Move clears yank+marks on dispatch; copy keeps yank for repeat-paste. Shared runPaste helper in src/clipboard.ts so chord and chip use the same code path.

title: Bulk verbs operating on Selection
description:
Verb set that consumes a Selection:
trash, move <dst>, copy <dst>, rename <pattern>, open, open-with <app>, reveal, drag-out, export-list, untag <tagname>, tag <tagname>

Invocation surfaces: command palette, right-click context menu, keyboard shortcut.

Destructive verbs (trash, move, rename) require a confirmation modal showing aggregate: 'Will trash 247 files, 14.2 GB. Oldest 2019.' Modal has Confirm / Cancel — no 'do not show again' (this is the safety net for AI-driven flows in Phase 2).

title: Tagging epic: DSL-driven tags + bulk verbs (foundation for AI-assisted file ops)
description: Build a DSL-driven tagging system as the foundation of an AI-enabled file manager. Phase 1: pure-metadata predicate DSL → live tag rules (color-coded, visualized) → tag-algebra selection → bulk verbs. No LLM. Phase 2: LLM compiles natural language to selectors; user reviews tagged files visually, accepts/rejects, then takes bulk action. Storage: rules persisted (JSON), no match index, no override storage — manual selection tweaks are transient. See conversation 2026-04-22 for full design rationale.

title: Add × close button to all overlay modals
description: ThemePicker, Welcome, ConfirmDialog, OpenWithDialog, MkdirOverlay, TouchOverlay, RenameOverlay all rely on Esc / outside-click. Add a top-right × button to each so mouse users have a discoverable close affordance.
close_reason: See body. Theme picker: 200ms ready guard prevents synthetic-event bubble dismissal; cursor in ref; hover syncs cursor. Welcome/ThemePicker/ConfirmDialog: × close buttons. Brand: 'keyboard-friendly file manager' tagline added. Show/Hide hidden files: relabeled with broader aliases. PrivacyHelpDialog: 4-step Full Disk Access instructional modal that walks the user through before deep-linking to Settings; wired from Welcome 'How to grant access' + permissions verb. TipsChip: 10 rotating bottom-right hints, 9s rotation, hover-pause, persistent dismiss.

title: Tinted tab background by terminal state (color the whole tab, not just the dot)
description: In addition to the dot, the entire tab pill should pick up a subtle tint that mirrors the attention state so peripheral vision can read it without looking for the dot. busy=cool subtle tint (claude working), idle=accent tint (waiting for input), bell=warm tint (urgent). Active tab still wins visually; inactive tabs get the tint.
close_reason: Added per-state tab tint in Tabbar.css. busy=desaturated grey wash (Claude working, no action), idle=accent tint (waiting for input), bell=warm tint (alert). Active tab keeps its own pill styling but gets a 3px colored left edge so the state still reads at a glance. Complements the existing dot so peripheral vision can read tab state without hunting for the 8px circle.

title: Selection persists across folder navigation — should be cleared on cwd change
description: tab.marks is a flat path→true map shared across the entire tab's lifetime. When the user marks files in folder A, navigates to folder B, then runs 'delete', it deletes from BOTH folders because marks aren't scoped to the current folder. Two possible fixes: (a) clear all marks when the active column's path changes, or (b) when computing implicit sources, only include marked paths whose dirname matches cwd. (a) is simpler and matches user expectation. Apply on goLeft/goRight/openPath/navigateTo.
close_reason: Multi-drag: FileRow/FileGrid now take getDragPaths callback; FolderList supplies a stable one that returns all marked paths if dragged row is among them, else single. Drag toast removed entirely (only the cursor chip remains; chip already shows +N badge for multi-drag). Filter mode: removed ArrowRight openSelected and ArrowLeft goParent — Enter is the only action key now (matches the user's 'arrows never open' rule, applied here too). Marks: cleared on every cwd change (navigateTo, openPath, goBack, goForward, goLeft) so 'delete' can no longer pick up files from a folder you've navigated away from.

title: Mockup adoption — FileRow restyle (Fraunces name, per-type tint, on-hue selection)
description:
## Goal
Restyle `FileRow` to match the editorial row treatment in themes.html. This bead is presentation-only — no changes to selection logic, keyboard handling, or store shape.

## Owns (only this bead should modify these)
- `src/components/FileRow.tsx`
- `src/components/FileRow.css`

## Do NOT touch
- `src/styles/*` (tokens bead owns those — use tokens that already exist)
- Any other component
- Selection logic in `store.tsx` / `useKeyboard.ts`

## Depends on
- **mockup-tokens** (needs Fraunces via `--font-serif`, `--hi`, `--accent-soft`, `--accent`, `--rule`)
- **mockup-icons** (nice-to-have — if available, use `<Icon />`; otherwise keep current icon approach and let a follow-up bead swap them in)

## Changes (all from themes.html `.row`, `.row.sel`, `.row:hover`, lines ~224–242)

1. **Grid layout**: `grid-template-columns: 26px 1fr 100px 110px` — icon / name / meta / meta.
2. **Name**: Fraunces (serif), weight 500, 15px, `letter-spacing: -0.005em`.
3. **Meta cells**: Inter Tight, 12px, `font-variant-numeric: oldstyle-nums tabular-nums`, `color: var(--muted)`.
4. **Dotted bottom border**: `border-bottom: 1px dotted var(--rule)` — removed on the last row.
5. **Hover**: subtle underline on `.name` via `text-decoration: underline; text-decoration-color: var(--rule); text-underline-offset: 3px`. Background `var(--panel-2)`.
6. **Selected (`.row.sel`)**:
   - `background: linear-gradient(180deg, var(--hi), var(--accent-soft))`
   - `border: 1px solid var(--rule)`
   - `box-shadow: 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,0.6) inset, 0 6px 16px -8px var(--accent-soft)`
   - Name + icon tinted `var(--accent)`, name weight 600.
   - `padding: 9px 11px; margin: 2px 0` (slightly tighter than non-selected).
7. **Per-type icon tint** — apply a class to the row by entry kind:
   - `.folder-row .ico { color: var(--accent); opacity: 1 }`
   - `.image-row .ico { color: var(--hero-tint); opacity: 1 }`
   - `.film-row .ico { color: var(--accent-2); opacity: 0.95 }`
   - default `.ico { color: var(--ink-2); opacity: 0.7 }`

## Done when
- Non-selected rows render with Fraunces name + oldstyle tabular meta.
- Selected row has the gradient fill + accent-tinted name/icon.
- Hover shows dotted underline on filename.
- Folder / image / film rows pick up their tint.
- Dawn and Dusk both look right (test by flipping `data-theme` at `<html>`).
- No regression in keyboard navigation / selection state.

## Known overlap to flag
`fm-n4h "Motion: tracking selection pill in file rows"` — this bead sets the *static* selection style. If n4h is picked up afterward, it should layer on top (e.g., transform animation on the shared `.row.sel` rule). Leave the selection element structure simple to keep n4h implementable.

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — `.list` / `.row` / `.row.sel` rules (lines ~223–242); sample rows (lines ~375–386).
close_reason: FileRow migrated to editorial tokens: Fraunces name, Inter Tight oldstyle tabular meta, dotted rule separators, per-type glyph tints (folder/image/film/code/link/exec/archive), on-hue gradient selection with accent-tinted name, tokenized checkbox. Drag/mark/yank states preserved.

title: Phase 2: LLM-suggested tag name + color
description:
When LLM emits a selector, also propose:
- A short tag name (kebab-case, <20 chars)
- A color from the existing palette (semantic match: red for cleanup/danger, blue for work, green for archive, etc.)

User can override before saving. Small polish but raises perceived intelligence significantly.

title: Mockup adoption — Sidebar (Favorites / Locations / Tags with editorial styling)
description:
## Goal
Build the left sidebar from themes.html: sectioned links, drive progress bars, tag dots, ornamental crest. New component; slots into the `side` grid area reserved by mockup-shell.

## Owns (new files only)
- `src/components/Sidebar.tsx` (new)
- `src/components/Sidebar.css` (new)

## Do NOT touch
- `src/App.tsx` / `src/App.css` beyond a single import + mounting `<Sidebar />` in the reserved slot (ideally this line is already a placeholder from the shell bead).
- Any other component.

## Depends on
- **mockup-shell** (needs the `side` grid slot reserved)
- **mockup-tokens** (needs `--panel`, `--rule`, `--hi`, `--accent`, `--accent-2`, `--hero-tint`, `--chip`)
- **mockup-icons** (needs `<Icon>` for home/desktop/docs/download/picture/music/movie/drive/usb)

## Sections to render (themes.html:331–361)

1. **Favorites** — Home / Desktop / Documents / Downloads / Pictures / Music / Movies. Active link gets the `.link.active` treatment: `linear-gradient(180deg, var(--hi), var(--bg))`, accent text/icon, inset highlight shadow, accent-soft drop.
2. **Locations** — drive rows showing device icon + label + progress bar + "X GB of Y GB used" caption. The progress bar uses a gradient: `linear-gradient(90deg, var(--accent-2), var(--hero-tint))`.
3. **Tags** — link rows with a 9×9 colored dot (accent, hero-tint, accent-2 — one each).
4. **Crest** — a bottom `❦` in Fraunces, `var(--accent)`, `opacity: 0.5`, letter-spaced (themes.html:191).

Section headers use the H4 style (themes.html:173–175): uppercase 10px, 0.22em tracking, muted color, with a trailing rule that fills the remaining width via `::after { content:''; flex:1; height:1px; background: var(--rule) }`.

## Data source
Favorites list can live in a const inside Sidebar.tsx for now. Drives data is also fixed for v1 — wire real drive detection to a follow-up bead. Tags read from the app's existing tag store if one exists; if not, hardcode.

## Done when
- Sidebar renders in the reserved grid slot under both Dawn and Dusk themes.
- Active state flips on a click (can be purely local state for now; integration with navigation is a follow-up).
- Progress bars animate width correctly with gradient fill.
- Crest renders.
- Keyboard focus ring works on links (TAB moves through them).

## Out of scope (file a follow-up if wanted)
- Actual drive detection / hot-plug.
- Tag editing.
- Reordering / user-configurable favorites.

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — `.sidebar`, `.link`, `.link.active`, `.drive`, `.crest` CSS (lines ~172–191); markup (lines ~331–361).
close_reason: Sidebar shipped. Favorites resolve off fm.homedir(), buttons call navigateTo; active state tracks current cwd. Drive row is a placeholder (TODO for real detection). Tags derived from state.tags with colored-dot rows + readable aliases for common chars. Crest anchored at column bottom. Panel-ink aware for dark-chrome themes. Mounted into shell's side slot.

title: Real drive/location detection in Sidebar
description: Replace hardcoded Macintosh HD DriveRow with auto-detected locations: boot volume (statfs), /Volumes/* externals, ~/Library/CloudStorage/* cloud providers, and iCloud Drive. Parse provider names from CloudStorage dirs. Click-to-nav. Hot-plug via polling on window focus.
close_reason: Closed

title: Replace 'Restyle' button with verb 'theme' in command prompt
description: Currently the titlebar has a 'Restyle' button that opens the theme picker as a popover. Remove it and instead make 'theme' a typeable verb in the command prompt (alongside other verbs). Selecting it opens the same theme picker, so the picker UI stays — only its entry point changes.

Removed Restyle button + popover from Titlebar; created ThemePicker.tsx as a centered modal opened by the new 'theme' verb in ChipPrompt. Active swatch label now uses --ink (was --accent) so dark themes don't get accent-on-accent-soft. Hover state: filled bg + accent-tinted border + 1px lift, replacing the bare underline.

title: [later] Multi-split / quad-pane terminal layout in a tab
description: Once single-split feel is right, allow multiple terminal splits in a tab — vertical/horizontal/quad. Build a split-pane primitive (react-mosaic or hand-rolled flex splits) where each leaf is a folder pane or terminal pane. Drag-to-resize. Each terminal independently named/themed/attention-tracked. Tab-level attention is OR of all panes.

title: Selection from tag algebra (transient)
description:
Build a Selection from a tag-algebra expression: e.g. 'tag:cleanup and tag:large and not tag:keep'.

Flow:
1. User invokes 'select by expression' (palette / hotkey)
2. Engine evaluates expression against current view → set of paths
3. Selection displayed in the file list with per-row checkboxes
4. User can untick rows to spare them — this edit is transient (no persistence)
5. Selection evaporates on view change or after a verb fires

Reuses the predicate engine — tag:name is just a predicate atom.

title: Phase 2: refinement loop (rejected files → regenerated selector)
description:
When user rejects N files from a proposed tag's match set, send those N as negative examples back to the LLM along with the original selector. LLM emits an improved selector that excludes the negatives while preserving intent.

Critical that rejected files become input to rule regeneration, not stored as exceptions on the tag — keeps tags clean (single declarative rule, no exception cruft).

UX: 'Refine' button on tag with selected exclusions; LLM proposes new selector; user accepts/edits/rejects.

title: Rotating tips chip in modeline / statusbar
description: When the user is idle (no overlay, no recent action), rotate short hint tips like 'Want to find a folder? Start typing find'. Place in modeline or a dedicated chip; rotate every ~8s; pause on hover; persist 'tipsDismissed' so user can hide forever.
close_reason: See body. Theme picker: 200ms ready guard prevents synthetic-event bubble dismissal; cursor in ref; hover syncs cursor. Welcome/ThemePicker/ConfirmDialog: × close buttons. Brand: 'keyboard-friendly file manager' tagline added. Show/Hide hidden files: relabeled with broader aliases. PrivacyHelpDialog: 4-step Full Disk Access instructional modal that walks the user through before deep-linking to Settings; wired from Welcome 'How to grant access' + permissions verb. TipsChip: 10 rotating bottom-right hints, 9s rotation, hover-pause, persistent dismiss.

title: Notifications when start_at arrives (and optional near-due reminders)
description:
On Breeze startup and once daily (e.g. 8am local), check for tasks where start_at = today and not yet seen. Show a native macOS notification grouping them ('3 tasks starting today: …'). User opens Breeze to see them.

Optional: same daily check for tasks due tomorrow (configurable in Settings: off / start-only / start+near-due). Default: start-only.

Track 'last_notified_for_date' per task to avoid re-firing within the same day. Settings toggle to disable globally.

Must survive across app restarts — store last-checked date in app prefs.

title: Tag verbs: create-tag, apply-tag, untag (manual tagging surface)
description:
Verb-driven manual tagging via the chip palette. Complements the rule-based tags from fm-uns: not every useful tag is a one-line predicate, so users can also create a manual tag (name + color) and explicitly apply/remove it on selected files.

Verbs:
- newtag: opens a small overlay prompting for name + color swatch; persists a custom tag in localStorage
- tag (apply): slot picks an existing tag, adds focused + marked paths to its manual set
- untag: slot picks an existing tag, removes focused + marked paths

Data: custom tags + manual path map persisted in main Persisted store. Match logic in tags.ts unified — tag matches entry if predicate(e) OR manualPaths[id].includes(e.path).

Scope: seed tags remain hardcoded (predicate-only). Custom tags v1 are manual-only. Predicate-driven custom tags wait for fm-j80 (DSL parser).

title: Consolidate search/filter into single Find button next to Views
description:
Current state: three overlapping ways to find/filter files — (1) search input in top bar, (2) filter mechanism, (3) '/' opens a weird panel at the bottom. This is confusing and redundant.

Desired state:
- Single 'Find' button placed next to the Views button.
- Clicking it enters the find/goto flow directly.
- Remove the top-bar search input.
- Remove '/'-opens-filter-panel behavior.
- Keyboard shortcuts '/' and Cmd+F both enter find directly (same flow as clicking the button).

Why: reduce UI surface area and mental overhead — one discoverable entry point for find, with power-user shortcuts reaching the same flow.
close_reason: Closed

title: Sort: add 'type'/'kind'/'filetype' as synonyms for extension
description: Sort chip currently only accepts 'extension' for type-based sort. Add 'type', 'kind', 'filetype', 'file type' as synonyms that map to the same sort key. Update the command palette so it surfaces 'type' when the user starts typing it.
close_reason: Added option-level aliases to ChipPrompt; 'By extension' sort now matches 'type', 'kind', 'filetype', 'file type', 'ext' in addition to 'extension'.

title: Sidebar Active Tasks section (above favorites)
description:
New section at top of Sidebar.tsx, above the existing favorites/bookmarks. Shows ACTIVE tasks only:
  status IN ('pending','in_progress')
  AND (start_at IS NULL OR start_at <= today)
  AND status != 'done'

Order: pinned first, then by due_at ascending (NULLs last), then created_at desc. Cap at ~10 with a 'See all (N)' link to the Tasks page.

Each row: title (truncated), small folder name (basename only), due indicator (e.g. red if overdue, amber if today, neutral otherwise). Click → 'go to task' = navigates current tab to the task's folder. Right-click context menu: Edit, Mark done, Pin/unpin, Snooze (push due +1d), Delete.

Live-updates on tasks:* IPC events. No polling.
close_reason: implemented

title: MCP server exposing tasks as tools for Claude/Codex/Gemini
description:
Wrap the HTTP API surface as Model Context Protocol tools so AI CLIs can call them natively without shelling out. Build only after HTTP + CLI have proven the workflow.

Two delivery options to evaluate:
1. Embedded MCP server in the Electron main process, alongside the HTTP server. Same auth model.
2. Standalone `breeze-mcp` binary (similar to the CLI) that talks to the local HTTP server and exposes MCP tools to whichever client launched it. Easier to install in MCP client configs (Claude Desktop, Codex config, etc.).

Option 2 is likely better — MCP clients expect to spawn an MCP server as a subprocess. Lower coupling.

Tools to expose: same surface as the CLI (task_add, task_list, task_done, task_open, etc.). Each tool input schema mirrors the HTTP body shapes.

Out of scope until HTTP+CLI ship. Linked here so the design is captured.
close_reason: implemented

description: Add Compress verb to chip prompt. Zips selection (or cursor item) into cwd. Single item -> <name>.zip; multi -> Archive.zip with timestamp suffix if collision. Uses macOS ditto -c -k --sequesterRsrc for resource-fork fidelity. Updates folder after write so new .zip appears and is cursored.

description: In ChipPrompt.tsx VERBS, the 'create' verb has type 'folder' wired to api.openMkdir() but type 'file' is no-op (comment says 'wire to a touch overlay if/when it exists'). title: Snooze + shift-due helpers; orphaned-folder reconciliation
description:
Two small but high-value affordances:

1. Snooze keybinds on task rows: '[' shift due -1d, ']' shift due +1d, 'w' shift +7d. From sidebar or tasks page. No dialog. Show a brief toast confirming new date.

2. Orphaned folder handling. On task display, if task.folder doesn't exist (fs.access fails), mark as orphaned in UI (warning chip) without deleting. Reconcile lazily — only check folders that are currently being shown. Surface in the Tasks page filter ('orphaned'). User can edit the task to fix the path, or close it.

Orphan detection runs on render, debounced, results cached for the session. Don't background-scan all tasks.

close_reason: Mark/yank state transitions shipped in 5031234. .row--marked runs gpChipFlash one-shot on first mark so the accent-soft bg eases in; .row--yanked has transition on opacity/font-style via --motion-toggle so yank reads as lifted rather than a snap restyle. Shares motion.css primitives with fm-z1f/fm-9q6.

title: DMG-installed app prompts for folder permission on every navigation
description: When the user installs the unsigned DMG and runs it from /Applications, macOS TCC prompts for Documents / Desktop / Downloads / etc. on every folder access — and search is blocked until granted. Possible causes: (a) quarantine attribute is set on the manually-installed app (the Homebrew cask strips it via xattr -cr; direct DMG install does not), (b) missing entitlements for files.user-selected.read-write or files.all, (c) Electron sandbox quirks. Investigate: 1) confirm xattr state on installed app, 2) add proper entitlements file referenced by electron-builder build.mac.entitlements, 3) document a one-line xattr command for direct-DMG users until brew tap is up.
close_reason: Added NSDocuments/Downloads/Desktop/RemovableVolumes/AppleEvents usage strings via build.mac.extendInfo plus build/entitlements.mac.plist with file-access + Apple Events entitlements. Awaiting next dmg build to verify TCC behavior. Preexisting FileRow.tsx TS error untouched (other agent owns it).

title: PasteChip: confirm + flip to success color + auto-dismiss; pulse pasted rows

PasteChip listens, flips to a filled-accent success pill ('Pasted N files ✓' / 'Moved N files ✓'), then auto-exits after 1.5s. For copy mode, yank is also cleared on success so the chip retires fully. The pasted rows pulse via celebratePaths() (gpDropSettle keyframe) — same one-shot vocabulary as bulk-rename / drop-settle.

title: pty data stops forwarding to renderer during Claude Code streaming
description:
During an active Claude Code response (active text streaming, TUI redraws, spinner animation), the renderer's fm.onTermData listener receives ZERO events. Confirmed via console.debug instrumentation in src/App.tsx attention monitor: a fresh `:claude` launcher produces 7 [attn] log lines as the shell prompt + Claude banner render, then the console goes silent for the entire duration of an active prompt-and-response cycle, even though xterm.js in the same tab is clearly rendering output to the user.

Symptom user sees: tab attention-tint (fm-4bs / fm-9mh) shows green during initial Claude launch, then stays red forever even while Claude is actively producing tokens. Tested with `QUIET_MS` bumped to 2500 and with the App.tsx subscription rewritten to subscribe-once-on-mount with refs (commit pending) — neither change produced any [attn] logs during streaming, ruling out React-side subscription churn or timer-based flicker.

Where the silence is: somewhere between node-pty's onData callback in electron/ipc.ts (~line 1499 `wc.send('term:data', { id, data })`) and the renderer's ipcRenderer.on('term:data', …) in electron/preload.ts:132. Two paths to investigate:

1. Main-process side: is the pty.onData listener still firing during Claude's streaming? Add a console.log at the wc.send call site (or stdout from main) to confirm. If main-side is silent too, this is a node-pty bug or alt-screen / flow-control issue with how Claude Code drives the tty.

2. IPC forwarding: if main is firing but renderer isn't receiving, the BrowserWindow's webContents may be dropping events under high frequency, or there's a frozen-context issue with the preload subscription.

Also worth checking: does the same silence happen with codex / gemini, or is it Claude-specific? Claude Code uses Ink (a React-for-CLI), which uses alt-screen mode + heavy cursor positioning; either could trip a node-pty path.

Files: electron/ipc.ts (pty + term:data forwarding), electron/preload.ts:128-134 (onTermData bridge), src/App.tsx attention monitor (~line 109).

Diagnostic to add next: temporary stdout log at the wc.send call in main proving whether main-side data is flowing. If it is, the renderer is the culprit; if not, dig into node-pty.

This bug breaks the green attention tint added in fm-4bs and the system notification in fm-c2w, since both depend on attention transitions reaching the renderer.
close_reason: No longer load-bearing. The fm-z7v hook-based busy/idle path bypasses term:data entirely, and the BEL/OSC9 detector that depended on it has been removed (Claude TUI emits BEL on every redraw, which was clobbering busy state). term:data is now used only as the byte stream into xterm.js; that path works fine. Reopen if a future feature needs renderer-side parsing of pty output.

Audit --ink vs --panel and --ink vs row-hover bg across paper/pastel/feminism/orchid/garden/linen/sakura/dawn/feminism-night/dusk. Adjust token values (not component CSS) to hit at least WCAG AA for body text. Also check --muted legibility for metadata.

All pairs now AA for text, AA-L for metadata. Feminism-night dark-chrome surface passes via --panel-ink by design. See commit 0f30491 for full before/after ratios.

title: Tabbar: task tabs visually distinct + right-aligned section
description:
Make task tabs read as a different class of object in the Tabbar. Two changes:

1. Right-side placement. Tabbar currently lays tabs left-to-right by creation. Task tabs render in a SEPARATE flex group on the RIGHT, with folder tabs on the left. A subtle vertical divider between the two zones. New folder tabs append to the left zone; new task tabs append to the right zone.

2. Distinct styling. Task tabs:
   - Different left-edge accent (e.g. solid accent color stripe instead of folder icon)
   - Slightly different background tint (subtle accent_soft) to hint these are operational, not browseable
   - Title font matches title hierarchy (slightly bolder than folder names which tend to be paths)
   - Show task title rather than folder basename

Keep all interactions identical (click to focus, X to close, drag to reorder within each zone — not across zones for v1).

Out of scope: cross-zone drag-and-drop reordering, "promote folder tab to task tab" verbs.

title: Local HTTP server + token auth in Electron main
description:
Localhost HTTP server in Electron main, the external surface for the Breeze app. Binds 127.0.0.1 on an OS-chosen port at startup; writes {port, token, pid} to ~/.breezefile/api.json (mode 0600). Bearer-token auth on every endpoint except /healthz. Cleans up api.json on quit.

Endpoints (v1):
  GET    /healthz
  GET    /tasks?status=&folder=&pinned=&search=&activeOnly=
  POST   /tasks
  GET    /tasks/:id
  PATCH  /tasks/:id
  DELETE /tasks/:id
  POST   /app/navigate           { path: string }       # nav active tab
  POST   /app/open-task-tab      { taskId: string }     # open or focus task tab
  POST   /app/launch             { tabId, launcherId, variantId? }
  GET    /app/tabs               # list tabs (id, kind, taskId, cwd)

App-level endpoints bridge to the renderer via ipcMain.send -> renderer dispatch -> reply. Use Node http module (no Express dep). Total budget ~250 lines.

title: Pinned folders in sidebar Favorites (verb + drag-drop)

title: Folder badge: active tasks count chip when browsing a folder

title: Tab attention badge: pulsing dot + state-aware indicator on Tabbar
description:
Render the attention state from the detection task on the Tabbar. Three visual states per tab:
- IDLE: nothing
- GENERATING: subtle non-pulsing spinner-style indicator (no urgency)
- ATTENTION: pulsing dot in accent color, 3-5s animation then steady until visited

State-specific hover titles. No new store fields needed; terminal.attention already encodes the three states.

Drop handler should add .row--dropped for ~400ms then remove it.

Likely cause: residual synthetic event from chip-prompt selection bubbles into the new modal's outside-click handler before paint. title: Tag store (JSON CRUD + schema)
description:
Persistent tag rules. Single JSON file at user config dir (e.g. ~/Library/Application Support/file_manager/tags.json).

Schema:
Tag { id, name, color, selector, mode: 'live'|'frozen', snapshot?: [path], created_at, updated_at }

CRUD ops: create, update, delete, list, get-by-id, get-by-name. Atomic write (temp file + rename). No migrations needed v1.

Why JSON over SQLite: human-readable, hand-editable, diffable, trivial backup. Migrate to SQLite only if frozen snapshots get huge or cross-tag queries get slow.

title: Motion: thumbnail fade-in on load in grid view

title: Task-mode shell: task header prominent, folder de-emphasized, filtered verbs
description:
When a tab has kind=task, render a different layout:

LAYOUT:
- Task header (NEW prominent top section): task title (large), status pill, due chip, start date, notes preview, edit button. Always visible. Replaces the current cwd display in the Pathbar position visually.
- Folder context (de-emphasized): a thin row showing the bound folder path in muted style — like breadcrumbs but smaller. Click to expand-temporarily, or to peek at files. Files list still rendered below but with reduced visual weight (smaller font, more muted) so it stays available without competing.
- Action zone (NEW prominent middle section): big buttons / icons for the high-intent actions on this task:
  - "Open Terminal" (just a shell)
  - "Launch Claude" (with task context injected)
  - "Launch Claude — continue" (resume previous session)
  - "Launch Claude — dangerously-skip-permissions" (yolo mode)
  - "Mark Done" / "Snooze"
  Other AI tools (Codex, Gemini) appear if their launchers exist.

VERB FILTERING:
- Hide file-management verbs (yank/cut/paste/trash/rename/mkdir/etc.) — the task is not about file ops
- Keep task verbs (edit task, mark done, snooze)
- Keep agent verbs (claude/codex/gemini, with their modifier options)
- Keep universal verbs (settings, help, theme, close-tab)

The verb filter reads tab.kind and the VERBS array gains a `availableInTaskMode: boolean` flag (defaults true; file-management verbs set it false).

OPEN QUESTIONS for design:
- When agent terminal is running in this tab, does the action zone fold away to give the terminal full bleed (similar to fm-jtu folder→terminal mode)? Probably yes — same "full-bleed terminal with attention badge" pattern, but exit the terminal returns to task action zone, not folder list.

Depends on: tab kind model (fm-???), Tabbar styling (fm-???).

title: Task context injection into AI launcher with editable preview
description:
When launching an AI agent from a task tab, automatically inject task context as the first prompt — but show it to the user so they can append/edit before sending.

FLOW:
1. User clicks "Launch Claude" (or any AI verb) in a task tab
2. Spawn the PTY with the launcher command and modifiers (fm-???)
3. Set BREEZE_TASK_ID env var on the PTY so the breeze CLI defaults work without args
4. After the agent prompt is ready, write a templated first message into the PTY input buffer (do NOT auto-submit). The user sees the context already typed, can append "and also fix the typo on line 42", then hits Enter.

TEMPLATE (user-editable in Settings):
   I am working on task {{title}} ({{id}}).
   Folder: {{folder}}
   {{#if notes}}Notes: {{notes}}{{/if}}
   {{#if due_at}}Due: {{due_at}}{{/if}}

ALTERNATE channel: write a sidecar file ~/.breezefile/active-tasks/{{id}}.md with full task as YAML+markdown so the agent can re-read context any time without burning prompt tokens.

DESIGN POINTS:
- Pre-typing into PTY input means the user has full control. They can erase the entire injection if they want a clean prompt for some reason.
- For non-task launches (folder tab launches an AI), no injection — bare prompt, like today.
- BREEZE_TASK_ID env var lets `breeze task done` work without an id argument inside that PTY.

Depends on: tab kind (fm-???), task-mode shell (fm-???).
close_reason: implemented

Strips Breeze entries from ~/.claude/settings.json hooks (matchers whose command contains claude-hook.sh) and deletes ~/.breezefile/claude-hook.sh. User wants filter to be pure narrowing — arrow keys should not open/enter anything. Only Enter on a folder should enter. title: Phase 2: metadata-only LLM tool surface (safety boundary)
description:
Implement the LLM-facing API as a strict tool definition: query(selector) returns ONLY metadata fields. No fs.readFile equivalent exposed.

Even if user later opts into content-aware features (hashing, semantic search), those must be separate explicitly-named tools the user activates per invocation. Default LLM context = metadata only.

This is the safety/privacy boundary that distinguishes this product. Document in code and in user-facing docs.

title: App-level attention: dock badge + system notification when Breeze backgrounded
description:
The cross-window unlock. When any tab fires ATTENTION and Breeze is not the focused app:
- macOS dock badge: app.dock.setBadge('!') or count of attention tabs
- Optional system notification with tab name and (if known) launcher name (e.g. 'Claude Code in ~/projects/foo is waiting')

Clear dock badge when Breeze gains focus AND user has visited each attention tab (or user explicitly clears). Notifications are opt-in via Settings; default ON for first install since this is the differentiator. Sound: opt-in, default OFF.

This is what makes Breeze better than tmux/iTerm for AI CLI workflows — no more alt-tab hunting through windows to find which session needs you.
close_reason: Dock badge + system notification when Breeze is backgrounded. Main process forwards focus/blur to renderer; renderer shows native Notification on rising edge from non-attention → idle/bell while backgrounded (clicking notification focuses Breeze and selects the tab). Dock badge shows count of attention tabs (excludes 'busy', which is generating-only); naturally clears as user visits each tab. Two new persisted prefs in Settings → Notifications: notifyOnAttention (default ON, the differentiator over tmux/iTerm) and soundOnAttention (default OFF). HelpTour catalog row updated.

title: Add ⌘F and / as triggers for 'find' verb (recursive search)
description: Currently ⌘K focuses the local filter pill (correct), and / opens FindPrompt which is also just local name filtering. User wants: ⌘F and / both open the 'find' verb in the chip prompt — find should be recursive search across current folder + subfolders (not just name-match in current). Implement recursive search in fm.findInFolder(cwd, query) (or similar IPC), wire the find verb to use it, and bind the keys. ⌘K stays as local filter focus.

Recursive-find implementation itself is still tracked under fm-8wf.

Add a left-side icon that reflects file kind: folder, image, video, audio, document, code, archive, app/exec, symlink. Extend the icon sprite (src/components/icons.tsx) with the needed icons if missing, and wire FileRow to pick the right icon via an extension→kind lookup. Keep icon small and subtly tinted with the per-type color already in FileRow.

title: [epic] Breeze-native tasks: folder-anchored to-dos with SQLite store
description:
Native task system tied to folders. Single SQLite DB at ~/.breezefile/tasks.db. Each task has a mandatory primary folder, optional reference folder, optional start/due dates (date-only, no time-of-day), free-text title and notes, status (pending/in_progress/done/cancelled), pinned flag.

Thesis: filesystem gives the big picture (projects, folders); tasks are the granular intent layer anchored to those folders. Tasks live above favorites in the sidebar (active only — start date passed, not done). A separate Tasks page shows everything with filters/search/bulk ops. Quick-add defaults to the current tab's folder. Folders show a badge of active tasks when browsed.

Decisions locked:
- Store: ~/.breezefile/tasks.db (NOT iCloud-synced by default; Export action for backups)
- Dates: date-only, stored as TEXT 'YYYY-MM-DD'
- start_at semantics: task becomes active at that date; hidden from sidebar before then
- Done tasks: kept forever; 'show completed' toggle on tasks page
- Priority: deferred; pin + dates encode urgency for v1
- Subtasks/recurring/reminders: out of scope for v1

Replaces the earlier idea of wrapping beads. Independent of the terminal epic but composes with it via a future :claude-tasks launcher (selection of tasks → templated prompt to claude in tab folder).

title: Motion: tab open/close/switch animations
description: Tab open: width grow from 0. Tab close: width collapse + fade using --motion-exit. Active-tab switch: animate the accent underline/glow sliding between tabs (shared-element style) rather than restyling in place. Touches src/components/Tabbar.tsx/css.
close_reason: Tab animations in 960636c. Opens run gpTabOpen (width + opacity from 0). Close via [data-closing='true'] variant that transitions max-width/opacity/padding to 0 — Tabbar.tsx schedules the unmount after the transitionend. Active switch uses a scaleX pseudo-underline on ::after so the accent bar slides between tabs without a shared DOM ref.

title: Sidebar click opens (or switches to) task tab; active-tab indicator
description:
Replace the current "click sidebar task → navigate active tab to folder" behavior:

NEW: Click sidebar task →
1. If a task tab for this task already exists in any tab slot, focus that tab
2. Otherwise, create a new task tab (kind=task, taskId=task.id, trail=[task.folder]) and focus it

Sidebar visual state changes:
- A task that has an open task tab somewhere shows an "active" badge (small filled dot or tab number "● 2") next to the row
- Clicking again is idempotent — focuses the existing tab
- Closing the task tab clears the active badge

Right-click context menu actions remain (Edit, Mark done, Pin, Snooze, Delete) — they do NOT need a task tab open.

The bound folder of a task tab can be navigated within the tab in task mode (the de-emphasized folder breadcrumb), but the tab is still semantically the task — not the folder. Closing the tab does NOT mark the task done; it just closes the working surface.

Depends on: tab kind model (fm-???), task-mode shell (fm-???).

The source rows should also animate to show 'these are being moved/copied' — e.g. lift + ghost outline + accent breath. There's already a .drag-toast and .row--dragging treatment (FileRow.css), but the user's perception is that nothing is attached to the cursor. title: Mockup adoption — metadata summary line (counts + type breakdown)

Extension→type map (images/docs/video/audio/code/other, coarse for prose readability). Renders under the folder headline. Pure renderer, no new IPC needed.

title: Tab management verbs + remove misleading tab numbers + fix active-tab contrast

Also fix the active tab's text contrast on paper-family themes (light bg + default dark-theme --fg-1 = unreadable light text). Use --ink fallback.

title: Typography variants for inverse / on-dark surfaces
description:
Some components render on dark or accent-tinted backgrounds (TaskShell title in some themes; future Project dashboard cards; bell-state attention badges). The existing --text-on-panel token resolves to the readable color *for the panel surface*, but components placed on tinted/inverse surfaces need a distinct color. Today we hand-fix per component (e.g. btn--primary hardcodes white).

Add to styles/buttons.css (or a new styles/typography.css):
- .text--on-accent { color: #fff; }  applied to elements whose direct background is --accent or --accent-strong
- .text--on-dark   { color: var(--text-on-canvas); }  for elements that explicitly invert in light themes
- .surface--accent { background: var(--accent-soft); color: var(--text-on-panel); }  the WAS-paired variant for tinted surfaces — same as btn--card-accent today but reusable

Update TaskShell title and any other components that report contrast issues on paper/dark themes. The .btn--card-accent variant should compose with these typography utilities so adding a card-accent button to a future surface gets readable text by default.

title: SQLite store + schema + migration scaffolding at ~/.breezefile/tasks.db

title: In task tab: clicking a folder navigates correctly (footer + sidebar)
description:
Two folder-click affordances on a task tab are broken or unintuitive:

1. The TaskShell footer renders the bound folder path as static text plus an "Open folder" button. Users expect clicking the path itself to do the same thing as the button (open the folder in a new folder tab and focus it). Make the entire path label a button with the same handler.

2. Sidebar folder rows (Favorites, Locations, Recents) still navigate the ACTIVE tab via store.navigateTo when in a task tab — this corrupts the task tab (it now has a different cwd than the bound task). When the active tab is a task tab, sidebar folder clicks should instead open or focus a folder tab for the chosen path; never mutate the task tab’s trail.

Implementation:
- Footer label becomes <button class="btn btn--ghost"> wrapping path text.
- store.tsx: add openOrFocusFolderTab(path) helper, or have navigateTo branch on activeTab.kind=task and route through openOrFocusFolderTab. The latter is cleaner — every existing caller of navigateTo gets the right behavior automatically.
- Definition of "focus": find a folder tab whose trail.last === path; if found, select it; otherwise create a new folder tab.

title: Motion: overlay enter/exit polish
description: Upgrade overlay animations in src/App.css. Current: .overlay fade + .overlay__box pop (scale 0.98, 4px). Exit: nothing — overlays just vanish. Upgrade to: --motion-enter for entry (scale-from 0.94, backdrop blur-in), --motion-exit (ease-in, faster) for dismissal. Requires two-phase unmount (AnimatePresence-style) since overlays are conditionally rendered in App.tsx. Applies to RenameOverlay, MkdirOverlay, QuickFindOverlay, ShellOverlay, Settings.
close_reason: Overlay two-phase exit shipped in 4e6dd3f... (see git log). useOverlayExit hook delays real onClose by ~180ms while .overlay[data-state='leave'] runs gpFadeOut+gpPopOut via --motion-exit. Wired into RenameOverlay, MkdirOverlay, QuickFindOverlay, ShellOverlay — backdrop click, Escape, and Enter-then-dismiss all route through exit(). Settings is a drawer (fm-8s9) with its own anim; its exit is a follow-up.

title: Zero-friction command overlay with inline completion
description: As the user starts typing (no modifier key, no leading :), a Spotlight-like overlay appears showing matching commands and natural-language intents. Supports two mental models simultaneously: (1) terse chords/commands for power users, (2) full sentences like 'move this to Downloads' or 'go to Desktop' for non-technical users. Completion is fast, keyboard-first, highly visible. Natural-language templates map to the same primitive actions as the chord system. Must feel delightful — animation, visual hierarchy, and teaching-by-showing when the user hesitates.

title: Mockup adoption — shell scaffold (3-column grid with named slots)
description:
## Goal
Scaffold the top-level grid layout from the themes.html mockup so component beads (sidebar / preview) can plug into named slots without stepping on each other.

## Owns (only this bead should modify these)
- `src/App.tsx`
- `src/App.css`

## Do NOT touch
- Any `src/components/*` file (sidebar/preview/filerow/titlebar/statusbar beads own those)
- `src/styles/*` (tokens bead owns those)

## Depends on
- **mockup-layout-decision** (must be closed first — this bead implements whichever option was chosen)
- **mockup-tokens** (needs Fraunces + Dawn/Dusk vars available)

## Changes
Build the grid from themes.html:142:
```
grid-template-columns: 240px 1fr 360px;
grid-template-rows: 48px 1fr 30px;
grid-template-areas:
  "title title title"
  "side main preview"
  "status status status";
```

Reserve empty slots for `side` (Sidebar), `preview` (Preview), and keep the existing MillerColumns or single-list in `main` per the layout decision.

Add the framed-canvas treatment to the main slot: inner shadow and upper-left accent glow (themes.html:194–210).

Wrap Titlebar in `.titlebar` grid area, Statusbar in `.statusbar` area. Leave empty `<div>` placeholders in `side` and `preview` slots with TODO comments pointing at the mockup-sidebar and mockup-preview beads.

## Done when
- App renders with a 3-column layout when window is wide enough.
- Reserved slots exist but are empty / placeholder-filled.
- Main area has the inset shadow + accent glow treatment.
- Dawn and Dusk themes apply cleanly across the whole grid.
- No regression in file navigation / miller columns behavior.

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — `.app` rules (lines ~142), `.main` (lines ~194–210), `.titlebar` / `.statusbar` (lines ~145, 278).
close_reason: Shell scaffold shipped: App.tsx/App.css restructured as a named-area CSS grid (title/chrome/side main preview/status) per fm-sdz decision. MillerColumns stays in main; preview slot is reserved but collapsed via --preview-w: 0 so post-v1 content preview is a one-var flip. Main slot wears the recessed-plate treatment (inner shadow + accent-soft glow). Sidebar/Preview left as neutral aside placeholders for fm-4zi/fm-fda.

title: AI launcher modifiers: claude continue / dangerously-skip-permissions / codex resume / etc.
description:
Make AI launchers compositional. Today fm-g6r ships a single command per launcher (e.g. `claude`). Real usage wants modifiers:

EXAMPLES:
- claude → bare new session
- claude --continue → resume last session in this folder
- claude --dangerously-skip-permissions → yolo mode (the user uses this often)
- codex → bare
- codex --resume / --continue
- gemini → bare

DESIGN:
- Extend Launcher schema in fm-g6r config with `variants: Array<{id, label, args, description}>`. Each variant is a named flag combination on top of the base command.
- ChipPrompt verb gains a slot when variants exist: `:claude` opens slot picker (Bare / Continue / Skip-Permissions). Default selection is Bare (Enter at slot picker = bare).
- Keyboard-friendly: each variant has a single-char shortcut shown in the picker (B / C / S). Type the char + Enter to launch.
- In task-mode shell action zone (fm-???), each variant is a button — click goes through the same path as picking it from the verb prompt.
- Variants are user-editable in Settings → Launchers.

DEFAULTS shipped with Breeze:
- claude: { Bare: [], Continue: [--continue], Skip-Permissions: [--dangerously-skip-permissions] }
- codex: { Bare: [], Continue: [--continue] }
- gemini: { Bare: [] }

OUT OF SCOPE: dynamic per-folder argument suggestions, history-aware variant ranking. Static config v1.
close_reason: implemented

title: Unified themes page with dropdown selector
description: Single HTML with theme dropdown; themes as CSS-variable sets switched via data-theme. Themes: paper, pastel, feminism, orchid, garden, linen, sakura, dusk.

Also: in the Restyle picker, the 'Feminism Night' tile shows label text invisibly against the dark swatch — the swatch label color needs to derive from the swatch itself (light text on dark, dark on light) instead of a single global color.

Active swatch label now uses --ink (was --accent) so dark themes don't get accent-on-accent-soft. Hover state: filled bg + accent-tinted border + 1px lift, replacing the bare underline.

title: Switch default layout to Finder-style (sidebar + single-list + preview)

title: Mockup adoption — Preview pane (photo plate, kind label, dl, tags, actions)
description:
## Goal

## What to render (themes.html:389–419)

- Wrapper: `.plate` — `background: var(--panel-2)`, `padding: 16px 16px 40px`, 1px border `--rule`, `border-radius: 2px`, multi-layer box-shadow with inset highlight + 18px + 4px depth shadows.

### 2. Kind label + title

### 3. Mini-rule with fleuron
Reuse `.mini-rule` from `ornaments.css` (themes.html:261–263).

### 4. Definition list (`<dl>`)
Each row: `grid-template-columns: 90px 1fr`, gap `0 14px`. Key (`.k`) is uppercase 10px caps in muted; value (`.v`) is 12px `var(--ink)` with `font-variant-numeric: oldstyle-nums tabular-nums`.

Example keys: Size / Dimensions / Taken / Modified / Exposure / Where.

### 5. Tag pills (themes.html:268–272)
- Default: `.tag` — 10.5px, background `var(--chip)`, colored dot.
- Favorite: `.tag.fav` — background `var(--accent)`, text `var(--accent-ink)`, dot inherits `var(--hero-tint)`.

### 6. Action row (themes.html:273–276)
- `.btn` — base pill, `var(--panel)` bg + `var(--rule)` border.
- `.btn.primary` — accent fill with `var(--accent-ink)` text.

## Data contract
Pass a `selectedEntry` prop with `{ kind, name, path, size, dimensions?, taken?, modified, exposure?, tags[] }`. The dl entries that aren't applicable to an entry type (e.g. exposure on a folder) should simply be omitted.

Image preview: render for `image/*` mimetypes only. Other kinds should render a placeholder block at the same aspect so the panel doesn't jump.

## Done when
- Renders for an image entry with full metadata.
- Renders for a non-image entry with the placeholder + only applicable dl rows.
- Tag pills render including a favorite variant.
- Buttons render (clicks can be no-ops for now; wire real actions in a follow-up).
- Dawn + Dusk both look right.

## Out of scope
- Actual drag source / drag-to-external-app behavior (owned by the larger drag-out epic).

`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — `.preview`, `.plate`, `.kind`, `h2`, `.mini-rule`, `dl`, `.tag`, `.btn` rules (lines ~245–276); sample markup (lines ~389–419).

title: Attention detection: cursor-visibility primary signal + BEL/OSC/activity fallbacks
description:
Detect 'tab needs attention' from PTY output. Layered signals:

1. PRIMARY: cursor-visibility transition. xterm.js exposes the DECTCEM state (CSI ?25l hides, ?25h shows). Most TUI LLM CLIs (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) hide cursor during generation, show when waiting for input. Transition hidden→shown after a hidden period = ATTENTION. This distinguishes long generation (cursor still hidden) from waiting-for-input (cursor reappears).

2. BEL (\x07) — xterm onBell event. Instant explicit signal.

3. OSC 9 / OSC 777 — explicit notification escape sequences.

4. Activity-stopped — fallback for tools that don't manage cursor. State machine: IDLE→ACTIVE when bytes_in_500ms > threshold; ACTIVE→QUIET when no bytes for >800ms; QUIET→ATTENTION. Tunable per launcher.

5. User regex override per launcher.

OR all signals. Three tab states: IDLE (nothing), GENERATING (subtle spinner dot, no badge), ATTENTION (pulsing accent-color dot + badge). User typing into terminal clears attention. Visiting the tab clears attention.

Verify empirically that claude/codex/gemini emit cursor-hide during generation before relying on it as primary.
close_reason: Embedded terminal end-to-end shipped in fm-jtu-terminal worktree. xterm.js (with fit/web-links/search/serialize/webgl) + node-pty in main process via @homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch (prebuilt binary, no electron-rebuild). :term opens a split pane rooted at tab cwd; :term-close confirms then kills. Theme reads CSS tokens (--panel/--text-on-panel/--accent) and live-swaps via MutationObserver on data-theme. Attention monitor in App.tsx subscribes to all term:data, dispatches per-tab attention (busy=cursor-hide, idle=cursor-show, bell=BEL/OSC9); Tabbar paints a small dot. Launchers loaded from userData/launchers.json (seeded with claude/codex/gemini); each becomes a chip-prompt verb that opens the pane and types the command. Caveat: tab-switch unmounts the xterm so scrollback is not preserved across tabs (pty itself stays alive). Multi-pane / quad-split (fm-5d9) explicitly deferred.

title: Launcher infrastructure: :claude / :codex / :gemini verbs + user-editable launcher config
description:
Plugin-style launcher system. A launcher is config:
  { id, verb, displayName, command, args, cwd, env, attentionProfile }
where attentionProfile tunes signal thresholds (quietMs, byteThreshold, regexOverride) per tool.

Ship with built-in launchers for claude, codex, gemini with sensible defaults. Each launcher's verb spawns a terminal pane (reusing :term plumbing) and runs the configured command in the tab's folder. User-editable in Settings — add/remove/edit launchers, override commands and args.

The 'plugin' framing: a launcher is just data. No code execution from user config (security). Built-ins live in code; user launchers live in launchers.json next to openwith.json/terminal.json. Settings UI to manage them.

Future extensions (out of scope for this task, but design for them):
- Per-launcher env injection (API keys from keychain)
- Per-launcher prompt templates ('start claude with system prompt X')
- Per-launcher attention regex
- Detection of installed launchers (which CLIs are on PATH) to hide unavailable ones

title: CLI fallback when Breeze is not running: direct SQLite reads + write journal
description:
The CLI must work even if the Breeze GUI is closed. Capture flows depend on `breeze task add "..."` never failing.

On every CLI invocation:
1. Try HTTP (api.json present and port open). If success, use it. Done.
2. If HTTP fails (no api.json, ECONNREFUSED, stale PID), fall back:
   - READS (list, show): open ~/.breezefile/tasks.db directly via better-sqlite3 in read-only mode. Same schema. Return results normally.
   - WRITES (add, edit, done, pin, delete): append a JSON line to ~/.breezefile/pending.jsonl describing the operation. CLI exits 0 with a hint like "queued; will sync when Breeze launches".
3. On Breeze launch, drain pending.jsonl: replay each operation through the same code path the IPC handlers use. Move the file to pending.applied.<timestamp>.jsonl on success (keep one rotation for debugging). Best-effort; if a write fails (e.g. id already deleted), log and skip.

Operation `breeze task open <id>` requires the GUI; in fallback mode it should `open -a Breeze` (launch the app) and queue a navigation intent for the app to consume on startup.

Concurrency: WAL mode on the SQLite db so direct reads do not block app writes. Document this clearly.

title: Rebind orphaned ptys after renderer reload (lost attention dot)
description:
Bug: after a renderer reload (Vite HMR full-reload, store-shape change, dev restart, or window reload), the main-process ptys stay alive but the renderer loses tab.terminal because tabs aren't persisted (store.tsx ~556-567). Result: Claude Code Stop hooks POST {pty_id, state:'idle'} to /claude-state; api-server fires term:fg via dispatchTerminalFg; App.tsx:154 findIndex returns -1 because no tab in the rebuilt state has terminal.ptyId === that id; dispatch silently no-ops; the green→red attention dot never appears.

Repro:
1. Open a tab, run :term, run :claude (or any AI launcher) — note BREEZE_PTY_ID baked into the pty env.
2. Trigger a renderer reload (edit a persisted-state shape in store.tsx, or hard-reload the window).
3. Tab strip rebuilds with a fresh tab and no terminal pane.
4. Claude finishes its turn — Stop hook POSTs idle. Nothing flips red.

Fix options:
- (A) Persist a minimal {ptyId, cwd, label} in localStorage as part of each Tab; on mount, ask main 'is pty N alive?' and re-attach if so.
- (B) On mount, ask main for the list of live ptys owned by this webContents and reconcile to tabs by cwd.

Prefer (A) — simpler, deterministic, handles the case where there's no surviving tab to reconcile against. Need to think about: stale ids surviving an Electron-main restart (pty ids reset to 0); show a 'pty closed' marker in that case rather than silently restoring a dead reference.

title: DSL parser & evaluator (predicates over file metadata)
description:
Pure-function predicate engine over file metadata. No file content access.

Atoms: name, ext, path, parent, depth, size, mtime, ctime, atime, birthtime, is_dir, is_symlink, is_hidden, mime
Operators: = != > < >= <= ~ !~ in glob between
Combinators: and, or, not, parens
Literals with units: 1MB, 1.5GB, 7d, 2h, now, now-30d, ISO dates
Tag self-reference: tag:name as a predicate atom (resolves to other tag's match set)

Output: parse(string) → AST; evaluate(AST, fileRow) → bool. Unit tested in isolation, no fs dependencies in the evaluator itself.

Defer: git_status, owner/mode, content hashing.
