title: Perf: stop FileRow from re-rendering on every store change
description: Every FileRow calls useStore() via React Context, so any state change (selection moving, mark toggling, filter typing) re-renders EVERY row in the current folder. In folders like Downloads with hundreds of items, arrow-key navigation feels laggy even though the per-row render is cheap — it's hundreds of renders per keystroke. Fix by making row-level rendering not subscribe to the whole store. Options: (a) split the store context into stable (handlers/dispatch) + volatile (state) and subscribe granularly via useSyncExternalStore with selectors; (b) hoist store access out of FileRow to FolderList and pass only the needed props (selected, marked, yanked, tag) which are already props; wrap FileRow in React.memo with default shallow compare; use useCallback for handler props in FolderList. Option (b) is more surgical. Verify by scrolling through Downloads with held arrow key — should feel instant. Profile in React DevTools if unsure.
close_reason: Hoisted store access out of FileRow into FolderList; FileRow + GridTile now React.memo with stable useCallback handlers. Selection moves re-render only the two affected rows instead of all visible rows.

title: Export tasks (JSON) + Import; basic backup story
description:
Settings action: 'Export tasks' writes JSON of all tasks to a user-chosen location. 'Import tasks' reads a JSON file and merges (by id; collisions prompt overwrite-or-skip).

Motivation: ~/.breezefile/tasks.db is local-only, not synced. Power users may want to back up to Drive/Dropbox manually, or move between machines. JSON is the lingua franca.

Schema for export: array of task objects matching the SQL columns, plus a top-level {version, exported_at} envelope so future migrations can reshape. Keep simple; this is for users, not for replication.

title: UI parity for non-keyboard users: command palette + menu bar + button affordances
description:
Make every verb reachable without typing. Three complementary affordances:

1. COMMAND PALETTE (Cmd-K) — primary discovery surface. Lists every verb with description, current keybinding, and category (Navigation, Selection, Tasks, Terminal, View, ...). Type-to-filter. Recent/frequent verbs float to top. Click or Enter to invoke. Same data model as the verb registry, so adding a new verb auto-populates the palette.

2. NATIVE MENU BAR — macOS app menu with verb groups: File (new tab, new window, open), Edit (cut/copy/paste, selection), View (toggle preview, list/grid, themes), Tasks (new task, tasks page, ...), Terminal (open, close, ...), Tools (claude, codex, gemini once launchers exist), Help. Standard mac muscle memory. Each item shows its keybinding next to it.

3. BUTTON AFFORDANCES on key surfaces:
   - Statusbar already has Help button — add small icons for common verbs (new task, open terminal) where appropriate.
   - Sidebar Active Tasks section: "+" button to add task.
   - Tabbar: existing "+" for new tab is fine.
   - Tasks page: header buttons for Add, Filters, Sort, Export.

Rationale: power users live in :verbs and keybindings. Distributing to a wider audience (even within the user base — colleagues who try Breeze) means many will never learn verbs. Without UI parity, those users churn at first contact. Cmd-K is the single biggest unlock; menu bar covers natural mac discovery; buttons cover surfaces where the verb is contextually obvious.

Implementation note: build a single verb registry that the palette, menu bar, keybindings system, and (later) help catalog all read from. One source of truth — adding a verb to the registry auto-updates all four surfaces. Avoid drift between :help and the actual verb set, which is a known maintenance pain (per CLAUDE.md help-system note).

This bead is the registry + palette. Menu bar and per-surface buttons can ship in follow-up tasks if scope grows.

title: Open With: picking an app navigates into the app bundle instead of opening the file
description: When the user invokes Open With and picks an app via the macOS file picker, the file manager appears to navigate INTO the .app bundle (since .app is technically a directory on macOS) instead of opening the original file with the chosen app. Investigate the OpenWithDialog → fm.open(filePath, appPath) flow plus the picker callback. Likely the post-pick flow accidentally calls openPath(appPath) somewhere instead of (or in addition to) launching with that app. Also: app:open IPC uses 'open -a <appPath> <filePath>' — verify spawn args order is correct and shell-quoting isn't munging the path.
close_reason: Removed treatPackageAsDirectory from app:pickApplication in electron/ipc.ts so .app bundles are atomic in the picker; user can no longer drill into Contents/MacOS and have open -a route the file to a nested binary. Added a defensive .app suffix check on darwin.

title: Mockup adoption — Statusbar restyle (selection count + kbd hint trail)

title: breeze CLI: thin HTTP client distributed via Homebrew tap
description:
A Node-based CLI named `breeze` shipped alongside the app via the existing personal Homebrew cask tap (or a sibling formula). Reads ~/.breezefile/api.json, makes HTTP requests against the local server.

Subcommands (initial):
  breeze task add <title> [--folder PATH] [--ref-folder PATH] [--start YYYY-MM-DD] [--due YYYY-MM-DD] [--notes TEXT] [--pin]
  breeze task list [--status=...] [--folder=PATH] [--pinned] [--search=TEXT] [--json]
  breeze task show <id> [--json]
  breeze task edit <id> [...same flags as add...]
  breeze task done <id>
  breeze task pin <id> / breeze task unpin <id>
  breeze task delete <id> [--yes]
  breeze task open <id>          # tells running app to navigate tab to the folder
  breeze status                  # is Breeze running, where is api.json, version

Defaults: --folder defaults to $PWD when omitted (matches the in-app quick-add behavior). Output is human-formatted by default; --json for scripting (for AI CLIs).

Distribution: bundled with the .dmg as a binary, plus a Homebrew cask post-install symlinks it into /usr/local/bin or /opt/homebrew/bin. Single-file bundling via pkg or bun build is fine; keep startup under ~50ms so it feels native in a shell.

title: Mockup adoption — layout decision (miller vs single-list + preview)
description:
## Goal
Decide the top-level layout for v1 before the team starts porting the themes.html mockup.

## The choice
The mockup at `design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` uses a single-list + right-side preview panel (3-column: sidebar · main · preview). The current app uses miller columns (n-column cascade, ranger-style).

Three options:
1. **Replace miller with single-list + preview** — closest to mockup, more editorial, loses ranger breadth.
2. **Keep miller, add a preview pane on the right** — best of both; needs proportions work because mockup assumes single list.
3. **Layout mode toggle** — per-tab or user preference; adds state.

## Why this blocks other beads
`mockup-shell` scaffolds the grid-template-areas for App.tsx. It needs to know whether `main` is single-list or miller, and whether `preview` is always visible or conditional. The sidebar and preview beads can proceed in parallel only once the shell is set.

## Output
Write the decision in `design-decisions/001-layout-choice.md` (create if not present). Include: choice, one-line rationale, and any proportions (sidebar width, preview width, breakpoints for hiding the preview).

## Done when
- Decision file exists and is checked in.
- Issue closed with the chosen option (1/2/3) in the close reason.

## Not in scope
- Implementation. This bead is a decision-only spike.

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — observe `.app` grid (lines ~142), `.main` (line ~194), `.preview` (line ~245).
notes:
## Decision: Option 2 — Miller in `main`, preview slot scaffolded but collapsed in v1

Adopt the mockup's 3-column shell (sidebar · main · preview + title/status bars), but keep MillerColumns as the `main` content. The `preview` slot is reserved in grid-template-areas and sized to 0 in v1, so we don't re-plumb the grid when file-content preview is un-deferred post-v1.

### Rationale
- **Miller is the product.** Ranger-style N-column cascade is what this project was started for; single-list + preview (Option 1) discards the core affordance for an editorial look we can achieve with chrome/typography alone. Miller's "next column peeks into the selected dir" already doubles as a directory preview — free.
- **v1 explicitly defers content preview.** Shipping a dedicated preview pane now (Option 1) means designing around an empty pane. Option 2 lets us ship the mockup's chrome immediately and light the pane up later by flipping one CSS var.
- **No layout toggle yet (Option 3).** Adds per-tab state and two code paths before we know we want both. Revisit after the preview pane actually lands.

### Implications for fm-dxl (shell scaffold)
- **grid-template-columns:** `var(--sidebar-w, 240px) 1fr var(--preview-w, 0)` — preview collapsed in v1; flip `--preview-w: 360px` (mockup value) when the pane ships.
- **grid-template-rows:** `48px auto 1fr 24px` (title · tabs+pathbar · main · status).
- **grid-template-areas:**
  `"title title title" / "chrome chrome chrome" / "side main preview" / "status status status"`
- **Named slots:** `title`, `chrome` (Tabbar+Pathbar), `side`, `main` (MillerColumns unchanged), `preview` (empty `<aside>` in v1), `status`. Sidebar/statusbar/titlebar beads unblock in parallel.
- **Breakpoint:** < 960px → `--sidebar-w: 48px` (icon rail). Preview hidden regardless in v1.
- The existing in-miller "preview column" (transient peek at selected dir) stays inside `main`; it is NOT the reserved `preview` slot.
close_reason: Option 2: keep miller in main; scaffold sidebar+preview slots per mockup but collapse preview (--preview-w: 0) in v1 since content preview is deferred. Unblocks fm-dxl with concrete grid-template-areas (title/chrome/side main preview/status) and named slots.

title: Fix image preview — cross-origin file:// blocked in renderer; register custom asset:// protocol
description: Preview pane image rendering is broken: renderer is served over http://localhost in dev, so is cross-origin and the browser blocks it even though CSP allows img-src file:. Fix: register a custom app-scoped protocol (e.g. asset://) in electron/main.ts using protocol.handle() that streams file bytes from disk with proper MIME type detection. Update bridge fileUrl() to return asset:// URLs. Also check that fm.readTextFile is actually being reached (user reports text previews also don't work — verify IPC handler registered + no errors). Test with .jpg, .png, .svg, .md, .txt, .csv.
close_reason: Registered asset:// custom protocol in electron/main.ts with MIME lookup and net.fetch streaming; updated preload fileUrl() to emit asset:/// URLs with percent-encoded segments; extended CSP to allow asset: on img-src/media-src/connect-src. Root cause was cross-origin block (renderer on http://localhost can't load file:// — CSP allowance doesn't override the web security model). fs:readTextFile IPC was already registered; text previews were failing from the same cross-origin dynamic when the page tried fetch() paths.

title: Motion: FLIP row reorder on sort change
description: When sortKey changes, rows currently teleport to new positions. close_reason: Deferred. FLIP row reorder needs a first-pass DOM measurement, a state swap, then delta-translate + transition-end cleanup — doable, but meaningfully larger than the other motion beads. title: [epic] Embedded terminal + AI-CLI launchers
description: Embed a terminal pane inside Breeze tabs (xterm.js + node-pty), with theme inheritance, attention detection, and a launcher system for AI CLIs (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini). Thesis: Breeze tab = project context. A folder pane and a terminal pane are two views of the same project, not separate tabs. The killer feature is cross-tab attention: when an AI CLI in a backgrounded tab finishes generating and waits for input, the user sees a badge on the tab (and dock badge if Breeze is backgrounded), eliminating the alt-tab hunt across iTerm/tmux windows. Primary attention signal is cursor-visibility transitions (hidden during generation, shown when waiting), with BEL/OSC/activity-stopped as fallback signals.
close_reason: Embedded terminal end-to-end shipped in fm-jtu-terminal worktree. xterm.js (with fit/web-links/search/serialize/webgl) + node-pty in main process via @homebridge/node-pty-prebuilt-multiarch (prebuilt binary, no electron-rebuild). :term opens a split pane rooted at tab cwd; :term-close confirms then kills. Theme reads CSS tokens (--panel/--text-on-panel/--accent) and live-swaps via MutationObserver on data-theme. Attention monitor in App.tsx subscribes to all term:data, dispatches per-tab attention (busy=cursor-hide, idle=cursor-show, bell=BEL/OSC9); Tabbar paints a small dot. Launchers loaded from userData/launchers.json (seeded with claude/codex/gemini); each becomes a chip-prompt verb that opens the pane and types the command. Caveat: tab-switch unmounts the xterm so scrollback is not preserved across tabs (pty itself stays alive). Multi-pane / quad-split (fm-5d9) explicitly deferred.

title: Render-time tag evaluator + row visualization
description:
On directory load, evaluate every live tag's selector against the visible file rows. Compute per-row tag membership in memory; do not persist a match index.

Visualization:
- Row tint by highest-precedence tag (precedence = user-defined order, default = creation order)
- Badge column: stacked colored dots/chips for all tags on the row
- Hover/inspect: show which tag(s) matched and which clause of each selector hit
- Aggregate header: 'N files match, total size X, oldest date Y' for the focused tag

Performance: memoize per render pass; re-run only on directory change or tag-rule edit. Acceptable up to ~50 tags × 10k files.

title: Settings: per-launcher visibility + a default in the task action zone
description:
With Claude / Codex / Gemini all launching from a task tab, the action zone gets crowded and pushes the task header upward. Most users will use one or two launchers consistently; the rest are noise. Add to Settings under a new "Task action zone" section:

- A list of installed launchers (read fm.launchersList()) with a checkbox per row labeled "Show in task action zone".
- One radio button per row labeled "Default" — at most one default at a time. The default launcher renders first / largest / styled as the primary CTA in TaskShell.
- A free-text input for a custom action label optional override (e.g. "Claude → "Help me here").

State persistence: extend launchers.json (electron/ipc.ts already handles read/write). Add per-entry { hidden?: boolean, default?: boolean }. TaskShell filters launchers where !hidden, sorts default first.

Out of scope: per-task launcher overrides; system-wide rebinding of variants.

title: Optimize TaskShell action-zone styling: less overwhelming
description:
The current task action zone shows one card per launcher (Open Terminal + Claude + Codex + Gemini = 4 cards). On a small viewport this dominates the screen and competes with the task header. Make it less heavy:

Options to evaluate (pick one or compose):
- Replace cards with a horizontal row of pill-shaped buttons. Default launcher (per fm-???) larger / accent-filled; secondaries smaller / ghost. One line.
- Keep card grid but cap at 2 columns and shrink padding/min-height; rely on Settings filtering (fm-???) to hide unused entries.
- Collapse non-default launchers behind a "more" overflow menu.

Decision criteria: the task header should feel primary. The action zone should be present and one-tap reachable but visually subordinate. Solo-default users (everyone after Settings filtering) should see exactly one prominent button.

Depends on: fm-??? (typography variants for legible inverse text on dark/accent backgrounds), fm-??? (Settings launcher visibility/default).

title: Mockup adoption — SVG icon sprite + <Icon> component
description:
## Goal
Port the hand-drawn SVG icon family from themes.html into a reusable React component. Every downstream bead (sidebar, preview, filerow, titlebar) will render icons via this component, so shipping this first lets them land in parallel.

## Owns (new files only — zero overlap)
- `src/components/Icon.tsx` (new)
- `src/components/icons.tsx` (new — the sprite + type definition)

## Do NOT touch
- Anything else. This bead adds only new files.

## Depends on
- **mockup-tokens** (optional — icons use `currentColor`, so they inherit from the color of the surrounding text; no hard dependency)

## Icons to port (from themes.html:285–304)
`search, home, desktop, docs, download, picture, music, movie, drive, usb, folder, image, text, sheet, film, drag, open`

All share: 24×24 viewBox, `stroke="currentColor"`, `stroke-width: 1.5` (some 1.3–1.6), `fill="none"` unless otherwise noted, `stroke-linecap="round"` / `stroke-linejoin="round"` where appropriate.

## Design
```tsx
// icons.tsx exports a symbol sprite mounted once at app root.
export function IconSprite(): JSX.Element { /* the inline <svg> with <defs> */ }

// Icon.tsx renders a <use>.
export function Icon({ name, size = 18, className }: { name: IconName; size?: number; className?: string }) {
  return (
    <svg width={size} height={size} viewBox="0 0 24 24" className={className} aria-hidden>
      <use href={`#i-${name}`} />
    </svg>
  );
}

export type IconName = 'search' | 'home' | 'desktop' | /* ... */;
```

Mount `<IconSprite />` once in App.tsx (reserve a single-line hook — this is the one place this bead may touch App.tsx, and only by adding that single line; coordinate with mockup-shell bead).

## Done when
- `<Icon name="folder" />` renders correctly at any color (via parent `color:` CSS).
- Type safety: `IconName` is exhaustive; TS catches typos.
- Tree-shakeable: only the sprite is shipped; no per-icon component.
- Demo: drop `<Icon />` into one place (e.g. a new row in Titlebar) to prove wiring.

## Mockup reference
`design-assets/inspirations/themes.html` — lines 285–305 (the entire `<svg>` defs block).
close_reason: Shipped SVG sprite (IconSprite) + typed Icon component with 18 icons (search, home, desktop, docs, download, picture, music, movie, drive, usb, folder, image, text, sheet, film, drag, open, palette) ported verbatim from themes.html. Mounted sprite once in App.tsx (single permitted line). currentColor so consumers tint via CSS. IconName union is exhaustive/typed. tsc clean.

title: Motion: pathbar breadcrumb transitions
description: On navigation, breadcrumbs should slide/fade in/out rather than rerender in place. When the search input is focused, crumbs can gracefully compress to yield space. Touches src/components/Pathbar.tsx/css.
close_reason: Pathbar breadcrumb transitions in commit cd80869. Each crumb-wrap runs gpPopIn on mount (React key by index+segment so nav re-mounts). Crumbs yield width to search on :focus-within via opacity + max-width transition so they don't clip mid-crumb.

title: Fix sort and view-mode chords not taking effect
description: User reports 'sort and views are not working'. The chord wiring (on/os/om/wl/wg → setTab) looks correct on inspection. Need to reproduce in dev and fix whatever is actually broken. Likely candidates: (a) chord handler swallowing keys before commit, (b) setTab patch not re-rendering MillerColumns, (c) grid view only applies to last column by design but user expected all columns.
close_reason: Added sort/view command aliases (date/modified/newest/etc), status feedback for all sort/view/hidden chord changes, and inline completion dropdown in command prompt

title: Drag-drop files between tabs and onto sidebar favorites (move/copy)
description: Make FileRow/FileGrid items draggable with the selected paths as payload. Accept drops on Tabbar tab buttons and on Sidebar favorite rows: default action is MOVE, holding Option (⌥) does COPY (matches Finder). If the drop target resolves to the same folder as the source tab's cwd, show a toast 'Already here — use Copy then Paste to duplicate in place.' and do nothing. Needs IPC helpers fm.move/fm.copy if not already present.
close_reason: Closed

title: Arrow-down can leave selected row behind bottom chip overlay
description: Pressing arrow keys to move the cursor uses scrollIntoView({block:'nearest'}) in FileRow.tsx:189. That call has no knowledge of fixed overlays like TipsChip (bottom:50px) or PasteChip, so the selected row can slide behind them while the browser considers it visible. Fix: add scroll-padding on .folder-list__body / .miller__list to reserve space above/below so 'nearest' always lands clear of the chips.
close_reason: Closed

title: Preview mode: hide sidebar, list-only rows, wide preview pane
description: When viewMode=preview, hide sidebar entirely, render folder as compact name-only list (no size/mtime), and give preview pane ~2/3 of screen width. Rationale: preview deserves most real estate when user explicitly chose that mode (wp).
close_reason: Closed

title: Share verb: native macOS NSSharingServicePicker
description:
Add Share verb that invokes macOS native share sheet (AirDrop/Mail/Messages/Notes/Reminders/extensions).

Implementation: ship a tiny Swift helper (native/sharer/main.swift, ~50 LOC) built at electron-builder time. Helper receives paths as argv plus screen-rect anchor coords, constructs NSSharingServicePicker with [NSURL] items, shows it anchored to the passed rect.

Renderer passes bounding rect of anchor element (FileRow for cursor, Pathbar Share button for multi). Main process spawns helper via child_process.spawn.

Runtime fallback: if helper binary missing (dev mode on unsigned build), fall back to AppleScript per-service for Mail + Messages; show status-line warning that AirDrop requires native helper.

Build: add Makefile step + electron-builder extraResources entry to bundle the compiled Mach-O binary.
close_reason: Closed

title: Frozen tag mode (snapshot of paths)
description:
Variant of live tags: at save time, snapshot the set of matching paths into the tag's 'snapshot' field. Subsequent visualization uses the snapshot, not re-evaluation.

Use case: 'the 247 files I tagged at 3pm yesterday' — stable target for cleanup reviews.

UX: toggle on the tag-input modal; 're-snapshot' action available from tag detail view.

title: Pink Feminism + Pink Orchid theme variants
description: Add two pink-family palette variants to paper theme family, cohesive (single surface + one accent + one secondary). Based on user-supplied palette reference.
close_reason: Closed

title: Motion: staggered row fade-in on directory load
description: When entriesByPath gets populated, stagger-fade the rows in using --motion-enter (~8ms per row, capped ~200ms total). Currently loads flash empty→populated. Important for slow/remote dirs. Touches src/components/FileRow.tsx/css — likely via a CSS animation with animation-delay computed from row index.
close_reason: Staggered directory-load fade-in shipped in 5031234. FileRow now takes an index prop, emits --row-i, and consumes gpRowFadeIn from motion.css (8ms stagger, capped at ~25 rows so large folders don't crawl). FolderList wires the index; MillerColumns skipped (dormant under Finder default). Reduced-motion safe.

title: Process-tree foreground polling for terminal busy/idle (replaces broken term:data signal)
description: Current attention detector in src/App.tsx subscribes to fm.onTermData and infers busy/idle from byte arrival in the renderer. Per fm-81n, term:data goes silent during Claude Code streaming, so the tab never tints green. Replace the primary signal with a 1Hz process-tree poll in the main process: for each pty, BFS descendants of the shell pid using a single 'ps -A -o pid,ppid,stat,comm' snapshot. busy = any descendant present; fgComm = leaf process name. Emit 'term:fg' on transition. Renderer maps busy=true→attention 'busy' (green), busy=false→'idle' (red), with bell still winning. Bypasses the IPC firehose entirely.
close_reason: Pivoted from process-tree polling to Claude Code hooks (UserPromptSubmit→busy, Stop/StopFailure→idle). Hooks POST to localhost api-server keyed by $BREEZE_PTY_ID env var (injected at pty spawn) so multiple concurrent claude sessions route to the right tab. Process-tree polling was implemented first but suffered the same fm-81n IPC silence as the original term:data approach plus noise from Claude's MCP children flickering busy/idle. Hooks are bulletproof: claude itself emits them, no terminal/IPC dependency.

title: Unify interaction to verb-first command system with visual checkboxes for selection
description:
Currently the app mixes single-key shortcuts (/, V, etc.) with the verb/sentence-chip command system. Goal: migrate all shortcuts into the verb system so users have one consistent way to drive the app.

Exceptions / special cases:
- Space remains the selection toggle (single item under cursor)
- Shift+Space selects all

Visual selection affordance:
- Add checkboxes next to each row so selection state is visually obvious
- Add a master 'select all' checkbox in the header
- Hover tooltip on row checkbox: 'Press space to select'
- Hover tooltip on select-all checkbox: 'Press shift+space to select all'

Verb-driven selection:
- Typing 'select' opens a suggestion menu: documents, images, videos, all, none, by extension, by pattern, etc.
- After a selection is made, flow should auto-advance to the next verb (action on selection) so the user can chain: select -> images -> copy -> [destination]

Scope of migration: audit all current single-key shortcuts (/, V, y, p, d, etc.) and either (a) replace with verbs in the command palette, or (b) document the few that remain (space, shift+space).

Acceptance:
- No single-letter action shortcuts remain except space/shift+space for selection
- Checkboxes render on all rows and header with working tooltips
- 'select' verb offers smart filter options and auto-chains to next verb
close_reason: Shipped verb-first model: added 'Select' verb with smart filters + auto-chain to next verb; removed single-letter action keys (v,f,s,R,a,A,I) - they now open the palette; kept motion + Space/Shift+Space for selection; added checkboxes on rows and a tri-state select-all checkbox in column header with tooltips teaching the keyboard shortcuts. Neutral styling via row__checkbox / col-head__checkbox hooks for design-system to tokenize.

title: Tag input UX (modal/palette to define a tag)
description:
UI for creating/editing a tag rule.

Inputs: name, color (swatch picker), selector (DSL textarea), mode (live/frozen).

While typing the selector:
- Live row-count preview against current directory
- Inline syntax error highlighting with caret position
- Optional: clause-by-clause match counts (helps user understand what each AND/OR contributes)

Frozen mode: snapshot the matching path list at save time.
